annotations.label: Formula (general)

anchor.snippet: a formula is a concise way of expressing information symbolically

annotations.note: An informal encyclopedic notion of symbolic expression with no mathematical content to formalize in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Formula in mathematics

anchor.snippet: a formula generally refers to an equation or inequality relating one expression to another

annotations.note: An informal description of formulas as equations/inequalities; Mathlib uses Lean's built-in Eq and order relations rather than a named formalization of this concept.

annotations.label: Volume of a sphere formula

anchor.snippet: the volume of a sphere in terms of its radius

annotations.note: Mathlib gives the n-dimensional ball volume in terms of radius (via Gamma), of which V = 4/3·π·r³ is the 3-dimensional special case (also InnerProductSpace.volume_ball_of_dim_odd).

annotations.label: Newton's second law formula

anchor.snippet: is an expression of Newton's second law

annotations.note: Newton's second law F = ma is a physics relation not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Expressions vs. formulas

anchor.snippet: Expressions are distinct from formulas in the sense that they don't usually contain relations like equality

annotations.note: A terminological distinction between expressions and formulas with no corresponding declaration in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Formula as Boolean expression

anchor.snippet: formulas are viewed as expressions that can be evaluated to true or false

annotations.note: Mathlib has formula realization that evaluates a first-order formula to a Prop (truth value), but no general Boolean-valued formula evaluation type matching this informal description.

annotations.label: Well-formed formula

anchor.snippet: is an entity constructed using the symbols and formation rules of a given logical language

annotations.note: BoundedFormula (and the abbrev Formula) is the inductively-defined well-formed formula of a first-order language L, a special case of WFFs over an arbitrary logical language.

annotations.label: First-order logic formula
annotations.note: First-order formulas (with the formation rules falsum/equal/rel/imp/all) are exactly Mathlib's BoundedFormula inductive type and the Formula abbreviation.

annotations.label: Formula in computing

anchor.snippet: a formula typically describes a calculation

annotations.note: The computing/spreadsheet notion of a formula as a calculation is not a mathematical object formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Boltzmann's entropy formula

anchor.snippet: An example of a formula used in science is Boltzmann's entropy formula

annotations.note: Boltzmann's entropy formula S = k·ln W is a physics relation not present in Mathlib.
